meaning: n.单片电路,单块集成电路
examples: Don't think this gang is monolithic — it can be split up.
Evidence such as this serves to undermine the apparently monolithic edifice of Victorianism.
Cuban-Americans no longer form a monolithic block.
It would be wrong to see a youth wave as a monolithic force for change.
When we say mainstream, we think of a single monolithic culture that dominates the conversation.
